key_insight: The paper adds graph-based row/column regularization to Riemannian low-rank matrix completion, improving the classical RTRMC formulation via variable projection on a Grassmann manifold.
engineering_relevance: This is relevant for recommender systems, sensor data recovery, and any application where missing entries can be inferred more reliably by exploiting known similarities between rows or columns.
method: Start from the RTRMC framework for low-rank matrix completion.
Introduce graph regularization to encode relationships among rows and columns.
Use variable projection to keep the optimization on a single Grassmann manifold.
evidence_signal: The abstract claims improved accuracy and robustness, especially when the data has strong row or column correlations, but it does not report specific experiments or numerical results.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the graph structure; if the similarity graph is inaccurate or weak, regularization may bias the completion result.
paper_title: Graph-Regularized Low-Rank Matrix Completion by Variable Projection
